chunk_summary: Guidelines for home nasogastric tube feeding in preterm/LBW/ELBW infants emphasize caregiver training, mixed-modality feeding, and clear weaning criteria to ensure safety and promote oral feeding skills post-discharge.
learning_objectives: Understand criteria for selecting preterm/LBW/ELBW infants for home nasogastric tube feeding.
Learn caregiver training essentials for safe home NGT feeding, including tube placement and troubleshooting.
Identify weaning pathways and escalation criteria for persistent tube dependence or poor growth.
key_facts: Home NGT feeding is suitable for infants with stable cardiorespiratory status and predictable feeding tolerance.
Caregiver training must include hands-on demonstration, teach-back, and explicit stop-and-seek-care rules for emergencies like respiratory distress or cyanosis.
Mixed-modality feeding protects oral skills: oral feeds first, followed by tube top-up to meet daily mL/kg/day targets.
Weaning can begin when oral intake exceeds 80% of the target for several days with appropriate growth.
Escalate to specialist review if tube dependence persists beyond several weeks or if growth remains poor despite guaranteed volumes.
reference_quote: Short-term home NGT feeding can prevent repeated admissions when an infant’s oral skills lag behind nutritional needs, but only when caregiver training and follow-up are robust. (UC Davis Health Neonatal and Infant Home NGT Guideline)
